title: Contrastive Sentence Embeddings for Scientific Search
abstract: We study sentence-level representation learning for scientific abstracts using supervised contrastive learning with citation-aware positives. The encoder improves nearest-neighbor retrieval and topic clustering while remaining compact enough for CPU inference.
summary: A compact contrastive encoder learns scientific sentence embeddings that help search and clustering.

title: Masked Span Reconstruction for Research Abstract Understanding
abstract: This work trains a transformer encoder on masked span reconstruction over research abstracts and section-aware segments. Learned representations improve downstream classification and similarity search on small labeled datasets.
summary: Self-supervised masked span learning yields reusable abstract representations for low-label NLP tasks.

title: Topic-Aware Embedding Alignment Across Research Fields
abstract: We align paper embeddings across machine learning, vision, and healthcare corpora using a topic-aware projection objective. The method preserves fine-grained similarity while reducing field-specific drift.
summary: Topic-aware alignment keeps embeddings comparable across different research domains.

title: Lexical and Dense Retrieval for Literature Discovery
abstract: We compare lexical ranking with dense retrieval on a literature discovery benchmark of abstracts and paper summaries. Dense retrieval improves semantic recall, while lexical methods remain competitive for keyword-specific queries.
summary: Dense retrieval helps semantic search, but lexical retrieval still performs well on exact terminology.

title: Cross-Encoder Reranking for Scholarly Passage Retrieval
abstract: A compact cross-encoder reranks top passages returned by a first-stage retriever. The reranker improves mean reciprocal rank on scientific passage retrieval with modest latency overhead.
summary: Reranking improves passage relevance after initial retrieval.

title: Query Reformulation for Research Paper Search
abstract: We investigate query reformulation strategies that expand short research questions into terminology-rich search queries. Reformulated queries increase lexical recall and improve hybrid retrieval quality.
summary: Smarter query wording helps both lexical and hybrid paper search.

title: Instruction-Tuned Summaries for Related Work Drafting
abstract: This study evaluates instruction-tuned sequence-to-sequence models for producing concise related-work style summaries from retrieved abstracts. Grounded prompting reduces hallucination and preserves citation intent.
summary: Instruction-tuned models can draft grounded related-work summaries from retrieved evidence.

title: Retrieval-Augmented Question Answering over Paper Abstracts
abstract: We build a retrieval-augmented question answering system that answers factual questions about paper contributions, datasets, and methods from abstract collections. Retrieval quality strongly influences answer accuracy.
summary: QA over abstracts works best when retrieval surfaces the right supporting passages.

title: Compact Generative Models for Scientific Note Compression
abstract: We evaluate compact encoder-decoder models for compressing long paper notes into short actionable summaries. Coverage remains strong when prompts focus on contribution, method, and limitation.
summary: Small generative models can produce useful research-note summaries when prompts are structured.

title: Chain-of-Thought Distillation for Academic Reasoning
abstract: We distill multi-step reasoning traces into a smaller model for answering methodological questions about experimental design and ablation logic. Distillation improves explanation faithfulness on held-out papers.
summary: Reasoning traces can be distilled into smaller models for method-focused academic QA.

title: Verifier-Guided Evidence Checking for Research QA
abstract: This work introduces an evidence verifier that checks whether generated answers are grounded in retrieved abstract spans. The verifier reduces unsupported claims and flags low-confidence outputs.
summary: An evidence verifier helps prevent unsupported answers in research question answering.

title: Benchmarking Long-Context Reasoning on Research Collections
abstract: We benchmark long-context models on multi-paper reasoning tasks that require comparing assumptions, baselines, and results across documents. Retrieval and chunk selection remain critical despite larger context windows.
summary: Long-context models still depend on good chunk selection for multi-paper reasoning.

title: Robustness of Scientific Classifiers under Topic Shift
abstract: We test topic classifiers on research abstracts under temporal and domain shift. Performance drops sharply when new terminology appears, motivating stronger representation learning and calibration.
summary: Topic shift hurts abstract classifiers when vocabulary changes over time.

title: Adversarial Terminology Swaps in Literature Search
abstract: We create adversarial query variants by swapping scientific terms with uncommon synonyms and abbreviations. Dense retrieval remains more stable than lexical ranking, though both degrade under severe edits.
summary: Dense retrieval is more robust than lexical retrieval to terminology changes.

title: Confidence Calibration for Research Topic Models
abstract: We evaluate confidence calibration methods for paper topic classifiers and show that temperature scaling improves decision reliability under class imbalance. Calibrated models better support teaching and review workflows.
summary: Calibration improves how trustworthy topic classifier probabilities are.

title: Vision-Language Retrieval for Figure-Caption Matching
abstract: We build a vision-language retriever that matches figures to paper captions and summary sentences. Joint representations improve retrieval for multimodal scientific assets.
summary: Joint image-text embeddings help retrieve scientific figures and captions together.

title: Multimodal Summaries of Tables and Abstracts
abstract: This paper studies summarization of research papers using abstract text plus table captions. Adding structured evidence improves summary specificity for experimental results.
summary: Using both text and tables can make research summaries more specific.

title: Aligning Diagram Text with Abstract Claims
abstract: We align diagram text regions with abstract claims using cross-modal attention. The alignment supports grounded navigation between figures and written summaries.
summary: Cross-modal alignment links abstract claims to diagram evidence.
